column1: # bootstrap/src/lower/INDEX.tsv
# Dependency graph for Layer 5 (lowering) substrate chunks. Each row
# declares one chunk + its tier + its Hβ-lower-substrate.md § citation
# + its imports (must be defined in priors). build.sh assembles per
# this order; future drift-audit verifies imports resolve before chunk
# lands. Mirror of bootstrap/src/infer/INDEX.tsv discipline.
# Design contract: docs/specs/simulations/Hβ-lower-substrate.md
#   §1   — LookupTy + LowerCtx primitives (state.wat + lookup.wat)
#   §2   — LowExpr ADT shape (lexpr.wat — coordinated tag region 300-349)
#   §3   — handler elimination ($classify_handler — classify.wat)
#   §4   — the lowering walk (walk_*.wat)
#   §5   — per-edit-site eight interrogations
#   §6   — forbidden patterns
#   §7.1 — chunk decomposition (this file follows §7.1)
#   §12.3 — sub-handle dependency order
# Per the eight interrogations on Hβ.lower structure (mirroring the
# infer/INDEX.tsv discipline at the lowering layer):
#   Graph?       — this file IS the dep graph for Layer 5 chunks
#   Handler?     — build.sh is the assembler-handler reading this
#   Verb?        — chunk concatenation is `|>` per row order
#   Row?         — each chunk's "uses" column declares its symbol-row
#   Ownership?   — each chunk OWNS its exports; consumers `ref` them
#   Refinement?  — Hβ-lower-substrate.md § cite IS the per-chunk
#                  refinement contract
#   Gradient?    — each row added unlocks one lowering capability
#   Reason?      — hβ_section + commit message records why each landed
# Tiers (dependency depth — Layer 5 sits atop Layer 1 runtime + Layer 4
# infer; see runtime/INDEX.tsv + infer/INDEX.tsv for those stacks):
#   4 — depends only on Layer 1 runtime + env.wat (no other lower-layer deps)
#   5 — depends on Tier 4 + Layer 1 runtime + Layer 4 infer (ty.wat)
#   6 — depends on Tier 5 + Tier 4 + lower-layer + infer
#   7 — depends on Tier 6 + ... per §12.3 dep order
#   8 — depends on Tier 7 + ...
#   9 — orchestrator
# Status markers (matching infer/INDEX.tsv):
#   ✓     landed
#   ~     in-flight
#   (blank) pending
# Future rows (lookup.wat / lexpr.wat / classify.wat / walk_const.wat /
# walk_call.wat / walk_handle.wat / walk_compound.wat / walk_stmt.wat /
# emit_diag.wat / main.wat) get appended as their chunks land per
# Hβ-lower-substrate.md §12.3 dep order. NO placeholder rows for
# unlanded chunks per the residue-tracker convention.
chunk
state.wat
